title: oligoneuron rigidum var. humile
intro: Oligoneuron rigidum var. humile, also known as Appalachian Ironweed, is a perennial plant native to eastern North America, characterized by its distinctive ironweed-like flowers and rigid stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Oligoneuron rigidum var. humile include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various health conditions, including colds, sore throats, and skin irritations.
